title: Town Shell Triage Desk
topic: mayor-level intake, recovery, and operator routing
skill_description: Classify town runtime signals, route operator requests, and return auditable mayor recovery actions.
review_reason: mayor reviews priority, owner building, recovery risk, and whether operator approval is required
quality_rules: Every output must name an owner building or explain why the item should be closed.
Separate real service degradation from cosmetic projection warnings.
Route risky actions to mayor/operator review instead of executing them silently.
skill_rules: Read runtime, daemon, and module-health perspectives before deciding.
Prefer reversible recovery actions with clear evidence.
Return unclear requests to an explicit missing-input question.

participants.title: Mayor Runtime Desk
participants.focus_area: daemon health, schedules, and recovery state
participants.perspective: The town shell must keep the whole system awake without confusing retries with progress.
participants.current_question: Which due or degraded item needs mayor action before the next tick?
participants.contribution: Provides daemon heartbeat, due schedule, stale worker, and recovery-loop evidence.

participants.title: Operator Inbox Desk
participants.perspective: Operator requests need classification, priority, and safe handoff before agents act.
participants.current_question: Which request is actionable, blocked, or approval-gated?
participants.contribution: Provides request intent, missing input, risk level, and response expectation.

participants.title: Recovery Review Desk
participants.perspective: A healthy 24/7 town must distinguish useful recovery from repeated churn.
participants.current_question: Which retry pattern should be stopped, escalated, or converted into a bug?
participants.contribution: Provides blocker patterns, retry history, and recovery acceptance criteria.
